task: Audit the Challenger causal traceback proof pack for unsupported major causal links, missing contradiction handling, weak source lineage, and overclaim risk. Do not restate the full report. Return only public-safe audit findings, required corrections if any, confidence cautions, and claim-boundary warnings.

context.summary: Truth Reconstruction Challenge 001 asks for evidence-preserving reconstruction of the Challenger disaster causal chain. The current proof pack derives a chain from NASA Rogers Commission public report chapters, appendices, hearing testimony, and embedded NASA/Thiokol records. Major conclusion: known SRM field-joint seal vulnerability plus cold-weather O-ring sealing risk, normalized through repeated prior anomalies, then converted into launch approval by flawed communication and management reversal, caused a right SRB aft field-joint hot-gas leak, External Tank breach, and vehicle breakup.
evidence_ids: EV-001 smoke from right SRB aft field joint
EV-002 flame plume and External Tank impingement
EV-003 right SRM joint seal failure
EV-004 record-cold launch and low O-ring temperature
EV-005 delayed O-ring recovery and blow-by
EV-008 NASA challenge and Thiokol management reversal
EV-010 faulty/poorly understood SRM joint design
EV-017 pad ice as context but not physical cause
contradiction_ids: CT-002 uncertainty treated as launch-permissive vs delay-permissive
CT-005 prior anomalies as warnings vs acceptable experience
CT-007 formal safety structure vs absent safety participation
CT-008 operational schedule posture vs strained developmental reality
CT-009 pad ice concern vs SRM physical cause
CT-010 qualification/bulk-temperature vs local O-ring condition
constraints: do not disclose Inside Voice internals
do not claim AGI, consciousness, black-box solving, or global cognition capability
do not promote unsupported causal links
preserve contradictions rather than flattening them
separate physical cause from decision-context factors
treat schedule pressure as contributing context unless evidence supports stronger wording
treat exact micro-mechanical variables as partly unresolved where source evidence is not conclusive
